failure_modes.text: Observers can mistake surface grouping for explanation and stop before testing whether the apparent pattern is actually causal.
failure_modes.source_quote: gestalt-principles-of-perception conflicts with representativeness-heuristic (representativeness heuristic) when surface pattern grouping is treated as causal proof.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test whether the perceived pattern is causal: surface grouping can look like explanation without actually explaining the mechanism.

failure_modes.text: People can privilege the perceptual arrangement that fits prior beliefs and miss equally real counter-patterns.
failure_modes.source_quote: gestalt-principles-of-perception conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when observers privilege perceptual arrangements that support prior beliefs.
failure_modes.mitigation: Actively search for counter-patterns that the preferred perceptual arrangement might be hiding; confirmation bias exploits gestalt grouping.

failure_modes.text: A messy execution space can remain unintelligible if nobody imposes a usable whole-before-part frame.
failure_modes.source_quote: The model has high leverage when people first need a coherent organizing shape in order to make sense of complexity rather than another isolated fragment.
failure_modes.mitigation: Impose a usable whole-before-part frame on messy execution spaces; without a deliberate organizing structure, the signal stays unintelligible.

premortem_questions.text: Are we seeing a real structural pattern, or only a visually satisfying grouping?
premortem_questions.source_quote: surface pattern grouping is treated as causal proof

premortem_questions.text: What counter-example would break the whole we think we see?
premortem_questions.source_quote: observers privilege perceptual arrangements that support prior beliefs

premortem_questions.text: What is the minimum organizing shape that would make this complexity legible without hiding important parts?
premortem_questions.source_quote: helping the mind see the right clusters, contrasts, and whole-before-part patterns quickly

heuristics.text: Start by naming the whole pattern you want the audience to perceive before layering in details.
heuristics.source_quote: Gestalt principles are strongest when perception, explanation, or design depends on helping the mind see the right clusters, contrasts, and whole-before-part patterns quickly.

heuristics.text: Use grouping, contrast, and sequence to make the intended structure visible instead of leaving the audience to infer it from fragments.
heuristics.source_quote: creating a clear, simple framework (a powerful Gestalt) is crucial

heuristics.text: Treat the first visible pattern as a hypothesis to test, not as proof.
heuristics.source_quote: surface pattern grouping is treated as causal proof

curation_notes.summary: Focused on whole-before-part perception and organizing shape rather than broad design-history detail.
donor_drops: Dropped broader donor-style elaboration in favor of three compact source-grounded intervention chunks per type.
open_questions: Relation richness stays in Wave 3; Wave 2 only preserves the intervention surface needed for preview substrate use.

allies: Wave 3 preserves the first-order ally surface where the raw source gives a clean current-vocabulary seam.
antagonists: Wave 3 preserves the strongest current-vocabulary conflicts and leaves weaker cross-model seams omitted on purpose.
structured_tensions: Wave 4 remains observational only; this batch does not widen higher-order source coverage.
